home.title: Balla Botond
home.description: Balla Botond - Engineer & creator with a passion for thoughtfully constructed, timeless solutions. Explore my projects, connect on social media, and discover my work in development and innovation.
home.keywords: Balla Botond, Software Engineer, Tresorit, backend developer, APIs, Java, Spring Boot, Node.js, Cloudflare Workers, security, encryption, portfolio

movies.title: Movie Ratings
movies.description: A personal project of mine — my own subjective ratings of the films I have watched, built into this site for fun.

vortex.title: Vortex Chat
vortex.description: A peer-to-peer Android chat application using WebRTC for direct communication. Features real-time messaging, room-based system, and cross-platform compatibility with iOS. Built with modern Android development practices.

forge.title: Forge Projects
forge.description: A pair of Rust-powered generators for animated GitHub README cards, ran on Cloudflare Workers. StatsForge renders a customizable top-languages SVG card, while StreakForge produces a contribution-streak flame card with a live leaderboard.

fcry.title: Fcry
fcry.description: A cross-platform file encryption desktop app built with .NET 10 and Avalonia UI. Encrypts files and folders with AES-256-GCM using a passphrase-derived master key (Argon2id) that lives only in memory, with drag-and-drop, folder packing, optional key-file two-factor, and auto-lock.

pomu.title: Pomu
pomu.description: A minimal macOS menu bar Pomodoro timer built with C# and .NET. Manages work and rest cycles with customizable session lengths and real wall-clock timing, tracks daily productivity, and optionally hooks into macOS Focus modes and phone notifications through native shortcuts.

emailservice.title: Emailing Service
emailservice.description: A self-hosted transactional email API built on Cloudflare Workers. Send emails with a single HTTP request through any provider: SMTP, MailChannels, Resend, SendGrid, or Mailgun. Includes API-key auth, full CORS, and 7-day delivery logs in KV.

taskmanager.title: TaskManager
taskmanager.description: A comprehensive project and task management application designed to help you stay organized and productive. Organize your work with multiple projects and tasks, track progress through three status levels, and monitor real-time statistics. Access your tasks anywhere with full-featured desktop experience and mobile summary view.

minecraft.title: Minecraft Server
minecraft.description: Private survival server for friends with custom plugins and a welcoming community. Runs on a Raspberry Pi 5 on a home network.

map.title: My Map
map.description: An interactive photo map built with Astro and Tailwind CSS, showcasing places I have visited and plan to visit. Each pin is tied to photos, turning personal travels into a visual journal you can explore.

website.title: Personal Website
website.description: This website - a personal portfolio built with Astro, featuring a modern design with internationalization support. Showcases my projects, resume, and provides a platform to connect.

movies.title: Movie Ratings
movies.description: A personal project I built and use myself to keep track of the films I watch. Every score is purely my own subjective opinion, presented through a searchable, filterable interface built into this site.

lifeStory.title: My Story
lifeStory.caption: Balla Botond - Hungary
lifeStory.pullQuote1: the feeling of creating something from nothing was electrifying
lifeStory.pullQuote2: quality, in all its forms, is worth pursuing
paragraphs: I was born in Budapest, the capital city of Hungary, where the parliament towers over the river and history seeps through every cobblestone. I grew up in Esztergom, a city just an hour away, where the historical significance and natural beauty gave me an early appreciation for craftsmanship, the kind of patient, deliberate work that stands the test of time. Walking through the narrow streets lined with baroque architecture and looking out over the Danube from the hilltop basilica, I learned that the things worth building are the ones that endure.

As a kid, I was endlessly curious. I took apart anything I could get my hands on: radios, old computers, mechanical clocks. I was driven by a need to understand how things worked beneath the surface, and I never stopped until I figured it out. That same curiosity eventually led me to my first lines of code. I was around 12 when I wrote my first real program, and the feeling of creating something from nothing was electrifying. From that moment on, I knew that building software was going to be a significant part of my life. That same urge to understand what happens beneath the surface later pulled me toward security and cryptography. There is something deeply satisfying about building things that don't just work, but hold up when someone tries to break them.

Throughout high school at Árpád-házi Szent Erzsébet Gimnázium, I balanced academics with a love for fencing and music. Playing clarinet taught me discipline and rhythm, and it started my lasting passion for classical music. Fencing, on the other hand, sharpened my focus and strategic thinking in ways I still rely on today. These weren't just hobbies; they shaped how I approach problems, how I stay composed under pressure, and how I find structure in complexity.

After graduating with strong results, I moved to Pécs to study Computer Science at the University of Pécs. University life opened a new chapter, not just academically, but personally. I started building real projects, collaborating with peers, and discovering that my passion wasn't just writing code, but architecting solutions that make a tangible difference. Along the way, I met incredible people who helped me grow, challenged me to think bigger, and pushed me to become a better man. That environment taught me the value of surrounding yourself with people who raise the bar.

Outside the screen, I find balance in the outdoors, camping trips, and long walks that clear the mind. I've developed a taste for the finer things too: a good single malt whisky, a carefully chosen cigar, some beautiful classical music, and conversations that go deeper than small talk. These pleasures remind me that quality, in all its forms, is worth pursuing. They keep me grounded and give me perspective that carries over into everything I build.

Today, I hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science and work as a software engineer at Tresorit in Budapest, building end-to-end encrypted cloud storage trusted by people and companies around the world. The fascination with security and cryptography that began with taking things apart as a kid has become my profession. Every project I take on carries the same philosophy: build something meaningful, build it well, and never settle for 'good enough.' This website, and everything on it, is a reflection of that belief. It represents not just what I can do, but who I am and where I am headed.

experience.tresorit_desc1: Software engineer on the secure cloud team, building Tresorit's end-to-end encrypted cloud storage platform trusted by security-conscious teams worldwide.
experience.current_desc1: Built and maintained full-stack features for enterprise software serving major automotive clients.
experience.current_desc2: Worked with geospatial data using PostgreSQL + PostGIS to power location-based features.
experience.intern_desc1: Developed React frontend and Java/Spring Boot backend modules, adhering to industrial-grade coding standards.

education.title: Education
education.university_desc1: Earned a comprehensive Computer Science degree with a curriculum focused on software engineering, data structures, and advanced algorithms.
education.university_desc2: Graduated in 2026, with practical coursework and projects that bridged theory with real-world application.
education.drivers_license_desc: Obtained a Category B driver's license.
education.video_cert_desc: Completed a professional video editing certificate.
education.highschool_desc: Graduated with excellent results, establishing a strong foundation in mathematics and analytical thinking.

portfolio.heroDescription2: I build backend systems and APIs, with a real soft spot for security and encryption.

quote.text: "Quality is never accidental; it is always the result of high intention, sincere effort, intelligent direction and skillful execution."
quote.author: William A. Foster
